topic.name: Movement records and submission duties
topic.description: More realistic comparison with uneven drafting style and mixed obligation patterns.

cluster.name: traceability
cluster.description: Traceability, record retention, and reporting workflow controls.

sources.title: Consolidated EU movement controls (extract)
sources.text: Article 12(1). Operators must keep a digital movement register and upload each dispatch entry within 24 hours; where the platform is unavailable, a paper log may be kept and transcribed within 48 hours. Article 12(3). The competent authority may inspect records on request. Recital note (non-binding): operators are encouraged to retain backup scans.

sources.title: UK retained movement controls (extract)
sources.text: Regulation 12(1) Keepers must retain movement records for 5 years and submit a monthly return to the authority no later than the 10th day of the following month. Regulation 12(2) The appropriate authority may inspect records on request. Transitional note: until 31 March 2027, small operators may keep paper-only logs.

narrative.title: Realistic divergence narrative
narrative.summary: This case intentionally includes mixed drafting quality, layered obligations, and two proposition pairings for comparison.

run_notes: Second demo case with multiple candidate comparisons and less tidy source drafting.
